title: Julie's Apple Crumb Pie
instructions: Mix butter and brown sugar until well combined.
Add remaining nut crust ingredients (flours, walnuts, vanilla, salt, and baking soda) and mix until crumbly.
Press mixture along sides and bottom of a 9 inch pie plate that has been lightly sprayed with Pam.
Set crust aside while preparing apple pie ingredients.
Mix ½ cup sugar with cinnamon in a small bowl.
Mix ½ cup sugar and ¾ cup white flour in a separate bowl.
Cut in ⅓ cup softened butter into the flour and sugar mixture.
Arrange half of the apples in the nut crust.
Sprinkle half of the sugar and cinnamon mixture over the apples.
Arrange remaining apples on top (should be very tall).
Sprinkle with the remaining sugar and cinnamon mixture.
Top the apples with the sugar and flour mixture.
Bake at 450 degrees for 10 minutes, then decrease oven temperature to 350 degrees and bake for an additional 45 minutes.
Serve warm with vanilla or cinnamon ice cream.
notes: This recipe is adapted from Aunt Joanne's recipe.
Use refrigerated apples as they cut easier when cold.
Pair with vanilla or cinnamon ice cream for serving.
Julie recommends Cinnamoo Ice Cream from Maggie Moo's if available.
